# Expected files after /caveman runs
#
# NONE. /caveman is a read-only communication-style control — it changes the
# style of responses for the session and returns terse output to stdout. It MUST
# NOT create or modify project files.
#
# Presence of ANY file in the output dir after a /caveman run is a contract
# violation. Verify manually:
#
#   cd tests/fixtures/fixture-26-caveman/output/
#   ls -la       # should show only . and .. (plus optional `.rubric-status`)
#
# /caveman MUST NOT:
#   - Write / Edit project files (it's a style control only)
#   - Perform any global/network install of the upstream Caveman plugin, or edit
#     shell profiles / agent config, without explicit user approval
#   - Compress away security warnings, destructive/production confirmations,
#     verification evidence, gate status, blockers, or the skill-decision line
#   - Rewrite code blocks, quoted errors, URLs, paths, or commands for style
#
# /caveman MAY:
#   - Switch intensity (lite / full / ultra / wenyan-lite / wenyan-full /
#     wenyan-ultra) and revert on `normal mode` / `stop caveman`
#   - Drop articles, filler, pleasantries, and hedging; use fragments
#   - Preserve the user's dominant language (compress the style, not the language)
#
# Validation deferred (status: pending) — same bucket as fixture-15-advisor and
# fixture-21-mcp-docs (read-only, stdout/style flow). Manual verification via notes.md.
